antibot_fingerprint_sdks.name: Castle.io
antibot_fingerprint_sdks.evidence: classes7.dex: 39x io/castle/android, createRequestToken, X-Castle-Request-Token, castle.io; classes6.dex: createRequestToken/RequestToken refs
antibot_fingerprint_sdks.likely_version: 2.9.0 (string '2.9.0' in classes7.dex) — NEWER than open-source castleio-gen (covers v2.6.0/token v11, excludes v2.6.2+/V12). Algorithm may differ — MUST verify.

libbarhopper_v3.so / libopencv_java4.so / libtensorflowlite_jni.so (ML Kit / vision — ID scan)
ssl_pinning: unknown yet — check OkHttp CertificatePinner / NetworkSecurityConfig in Phase 3/4
rasp: rootbeer (Castle dep) + libtoolChecker.so suspected — confirm in Phase 4
analysis_chain_decision: Castle token gen lives in native Java io.castle.android → standard jadx Java chain (android-java-sign style). Hermes bundle NOT needed for Castle (it would only matter if the token were built in JS).

findings.detail: header X-Castle-Request-Token sent to DailyPay backend

open_questions: Exact Castle SDK version (2.9.0?) and token version (v11 vs V12+)
Is token built purely in Java or does it call a .so?
Castle publishable key (pk) value — where configured (manifest meta-data / JS bundle / string res)?
Does the v48 algorithm match open-source castleio-gen, or has the container/fingerprint changed?
